comments.author: @havaianuu
comments.text:
Yes, Saudade is about the nostalgic, deep and longing feeling that one has for their homeland, but it's much more than that. It also applies to anything that is near and dear to one's hearth, like an away loved one, a parent, a dear pet, etc. Also not understood by many, it's a bitter sweet feeling, that one embraces for the sadness of the absence of something or someone is compensated by the warm feeling and being thankful of having something to be nostalgic and longing for.
Usually Saudade is used in the plural form Saudades and in my opinion is one of the top two most beautiful words, in the few languages I can speak, for it expresses multi faceted feelings that hardly any single word, in other languages, can express, the other is Aloha

comments.text: My ancestors from São Miguel, started in 1828, when an ENGLISHMAN settled there, being born in London in 1804, after his marriage to a woman of that region. They had a son that migrated to the USA in 1888, settling first in Massachuetts and then California.The family grew up primarily in Farming areas of California. Have the records, great feeling watching that video. Never been there, but who knows...
